user_query:
I’m preparing to query a couple of Uniswap v4 pools on QELT Mainnet and to check some WQELT balances. Please do the following using only files in this workspace:

1) Read input/pools.json. For each item:
- Canonicalize the pool key so currency0 is the numerically lower address and currency1 is the higher.
- If hooks is null or missing, use the zero address.
- Compute the v4 poolId = keccak256(abi.encode(address currency0, address currency1, uint24 fee, int24 tickSpacing, address hooks)).
- Prepare two JSON-RPC eth_call request objects to the PoolManager contract for:
  a) getSlot0(bytes32 poolId)
  b) getLiquidity(bytes32 poolId)
  Use the latest block tag.
- Normalize all addresses in outputs to lowercase 0x-prefixed hex.

Write exactly one JSON object per line to output/pool_calls.jsonl (one line for getSlot0 and one line for getLiquidity for each pool).

2) Read input/wallets.csv (column header: address). For each wallet, prepare a JSON-RPC eth_call request object to the WQELT contract for balanceOf(address), with properly ABI-encoded calldata (function selector 0x70a08231 and the address as a 32-byte word). Use the latest block tag. Normalize all addresses to lowercase 0x-prefixed hex.

Write exactly one JSON object per line to output/wqelt_balance_calls.jsonl.

3) Also write output/summary.json containing, for each pool entry by its name, the canonicalized pool key you used (currency0, currency1, fee, tickSpacing, hooks) and the computed poolId as a 0x-prefixed 64-hex-char value.

Contract addresses to use:
- PoolManager: 0x11c23891d9f723c4f1c6560f892e4581d87b6d8a
- WQELT:       0xfebc6f9f0149036006c4f5ac124685e0ef48e8a2

Notes:
- Do not broadcast any transactions; only construct eth_call payloads.
- Ensure jsonrpc is "2.0", method is "eth_call", params has the call object with fields {"to", "data"} and the string "latest", and include an integer id.
- All outputs must be strictly valid JSON with lowercase hex addresses.
